summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on October 8, 2024. A major portion of the meeting featured a presentation by Steve Pegram, treasurer of the Goshen Fire and EMS 2024 levy campaign, explaining the critical importance of the replacement levy on the November 5th ballot, which funds over 60% of fire and EMS operations. The board approved multiple donations for fire training exercises, accepted food gift cards from Mount Carmel Christian Church, approved the purchase of 10 AEDs using remaining ARPA funds, renewed property and risk management insurance with Highland, and entered executive session regarding personnel matters.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Sept 10 – Oct 8, 2024) – passed
Motion to accept donation of $180 in food gift cards from Mount Carmel Christian Church – passed
Motion to enter into memorandum of understanding amending contract with Medi-Count Management for third-party Medicare/Medicaid EMS billing – passed
Motion to accept $750 donation from Goshen Lions Club for fire prevention materials – passed
Motion to accept $750 donation from Goshen Chamber of Commerce for fire prevention materials – passed
Motion to accept donation of semi trailer from R&L Trucking and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
Motion to accept donation of semi tractor from City Servicing and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
Motion to accept donation of multiple cars from Phipps Towing, Dakota's Towing, After Warranty Auto Repair, and John's Auto and approve disposal via scrap after training – passed
Motion to approve indigent burial of Jack Thomas Sullivan with Evans Funeral Home – passed
Motion to approve renewal of Ohio Plan property and risk management insurance with Highland for 2024-2025 – passed
Motion to accept resignation of Shane Young effective 9/23/24 – passed
Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for Goshen Cleanup Day from $13,500 to $10,309.18 – passed
Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for admin chairs returning unused funds – passed
Motion to amend ARPA appropriations for administration parking lot and sidewalks returning unused funds – passed
Motion to appropriate $20,222.48 into ARPA fund for purchase of AEDs in township vehicles – passed
Motion to approve purchase of 10 AED machines from Stryker for $20,222.48 from ARPA fund – passed
Motion to approve Flags for Heroes on township property for Goshen Lions Club – passed
Motion to enter executive session at 6:43 PM regarding personnel matters – passed
Motion to grant part-time firefighter EMT Seth Daniels a six-month leave of absence – passed
Motion to adjourn at 7:10 PM – passed
action_items: Chief Myers and levy committee to continue community outreach about fire and EMS levy including speaking at community groups
Post banners on Route 28 clarifying that the fire station construction is not funded by the levy
Levy committee to address homestead exemption question on Facebook
Administrator Rose to find projects for remaining ~$3,490 in ARPA funds before end of year
Levy committee to distribute flyers and candy about the levy on Halloween
Historical society funding request to be discussed during November 2025 budget discussions using general funds
key_amounts: $180 in food gift cards donated by Mount Carmel Christian Church ($50 to police, remainder to fire)
$750 donation from Goshen Lions Club for fire prevention materials
$750 donation from Goshen Chamber of Commerce for fire prevention materials
$20,222.48 for purchase of 10 AED machines from Stryker using ARPA funds
$1,738.41 approximate cost per standard AED unit
Insurance premium increase of $17,563 (~14.6% increase) for Highland property and risk management renewal
$196,000 average home value in Goshen Township per Clermont County Auditor
$106.11 per year additional cost for average $200,000 home for fire/EMS levy
4.4 mil replacement fire and EMS levy on November 5 ballot
~$8 million new firehouse funded 100% by insurance and state aid, zero local tax dollars
~$3,490.19 remaining ARPA balance after AED purchase
Over 1,000 simultaneous fire/EMS calls per year requiring additional units
notable_topics: Fire and EMS replacement levy presentation for November 5, 2024 election
Station 18 reconstruction progress update – building permit pending, steel ordered
Catastrophic consequences if levy fails: 35+ layoffs, staffing from ~50 to 14, station closure
Extrication training event scheduled October 17-19 using donated vehicles
Purchase of 10 AEDs for township vehicles and facilities using ARPA funds
ARPA fund amendments returning unused appropriations
Police officer Shane Young resignation before starting
Indigent burial approval for Jack Thomas Sullivan
Flags for Heroes display on township property approved for Lions Club
Homecoming parade rescheduled to Friday; trick-or-treat October 31, 6-8 PM
Trunk or treat partnership with Clermont County Park District at Stagamore Park
Administration building lobby floor renovation completed
Leave of absence granted for part-time firefighter EMT Seth Daniels
attendees: Chief Rose / Administrator Rose (Police Chief/Township Administrator)
Steve Pegram (Treasurer, Goshen Fire and EMS 2024 Levy Committee)
Melinda (referenced, likely fiscal officer or staff)
notes: The meeting opened with prayers for the Rossig family's loss, victims of a tragic accident on Sunday, Hurricane Helene victims in North Carolina, and those facing Hurricane Milton in Florida. The fire and EMS levy presentation was the centerpiece of the meeting, emphasizing that the current levy expires at end of 2024 and failure would be catastrophic. The levy failed previously in March 2024, and the board reduced millage by 0.1 mil from 4.5 to 4.4 as a goodwill gesture. A similar levy failure occurred in 2012 resulting in staffing cuts and neighboring communities billing Goshen residents directly. Executive session lasted approximately 26 minutes (6:43 PM to 7:09 PM). The fiscal officer's name appears to be Melinda or Laura based on context but is not entirely clear from the transcript.
title: BOT 10-8-24
